meta.tagline: Women's fitness & nutrition — postnatal, pregnancy, strength
meta.stack: Single-file React 18 (CDN + Babel) · Supabase (Postgres + Auth + Edge Functions) · Vercel

snapshotNote: Live counts baked in at generation time. In-app live metrics live in the Admin panel.
summary: This report is the living engineering record for SEZE. It began as a brand + UI overhaul and grew into a full-stack functionality pass: fixing a systemic persistence failure (the app wrote to a non-existent 'profiles' table), closing security and clinical-safety holes, wiring real user data across every screen, adding a nutrition data layer, an admin panel, a reviewable feedback system, and a per-programme workout engine. Every backend flow below has been verified against the live database.

buildLog.title: Nutrition accuracy: fully-local curated food DB (no external API)
buildLog.summary: Replaced Open Food Facts / USDA lookups with a curated, macro-validated local database served as a static asset (foods.json, 526 items = 471 everyday foods + 55 UK fast-food chain items). Every entry passes the macro-sum gate (protein*4 + carbs*4 + fat*9 within 15% of kcal). parseFood and the in-app food search now resolve entirely offline — no network, no rate limits, deterministic results. This fixed noisy branded matches (a single tostada previously read 470 kcal; it is now 231 kcal). Also fixed a parser bug where size words were stripped before matching, so proper-noun foods like 'big mac' and 'medium fries' parsed as big*mac / medium*fries — a new scoreFoodPhrase() tries the full phrase first and only falls back to size-word quantity parsing when there is no strong branded match.

buildLog.title: Nutrition logging: meal selector, add/omit foods, real-DB accuracy
buildLog.summary: Choose which meal to log to (Breakfast/Lunch/Dinner/Snack); add foods via real search (curated + Open Food Facts + USDA) and remove foods before saving; unknown foods auto-upgrade from a flat estimate to a real lookup; removing a saved meal also deletes it from Supabase; bottom nav added to programme detail; workout exit changed to a clear ×.
buildLog.verified: Logged 3 items all to Lunch; 'starbucks caramel' → curated Frappuccino 380kcal; meal_type persisted.

buildLog.title: In-app admin panel
buildLog.summary: AdminScreen with Overview/Users/Feedback/Requests tabs; plan management (free/trial/paying); admin-only SECURITY DEFINER RPCs; feedback category selector powers 'most requested'; prominent Profile entry.
buildLog.verified: Anon denied on every admin RPC; plan toggle persisted; overview shows real counts.

buildLog.title: Fix: Progress/Programmes showed demo data to real accounts
buildLog.summary: Progress totals, weekly chart, streak, programme progress now from real rosa_sessions (new account = zeros). Badges earned from real activity only and persisted to rosa_badges. Programmes split into 'Your programme' + 'Explore other'; ACTIVE follows the user's real choice. 'Start this programme' now persists the switch.
buildLog.verified: New account shows 0 sessions / 0% / no badges; programme switch new_mum→booty persisted.

buildLog.title: Admin access + case-insensitive check
buildLog.summary: Added elvarosaflores@seze.app to the allowlist; is_seze_admin() now compares case-insensitively; admins get a 'Review feedback'/admin entry on Profile — no dev URL needed.
buildLog.verified: Non-admin does not see the admin card; admin does.

buildLog.title: Dev mode + reviewable feedback
buildLog.summary: Dev button restored (localhost always; production via sticky ?dev=1). Feedback works logged-out (anon user_id null; RLS blocks impersonation), with optional email and real success/error states. Feedback review screen with status triage. Workout-content engine (distinct sessions per programme).

buildLog.title: Backend functionality: real user data, safe nutrition, auth flow
buildLog.summary: AppData provider loads real rosa_users + today's nutrition + session streak. Home/Nutrition/Profile show real data. Onboarding upserts full profile with life-stage-safe macro targets. Auth: Sign Up→signup, verify→onboarding, sign out, Apple/Meta 'coming soon'. iOS 16px input fix; a11y.
buildLog.verified: Signup→trigger→onboarding save (2450kcal safe)→real targets→meal logging all persist.

buildLog.title: Backend fixes + security hardening + nutrition data
buildLog.summary: profiles→rosa_users (maybeSingle), onboarding macro targets, workout session logging. Security: fixed a SECURITY DEFINER view leaking all users' nutrition data, feedback-spoof policy, function search_path. Deployed nutrition-search edge fn + seeded 55 fast-food items.
buildLog.verified: Supabase security advisor clear except the dashboard leaked-password toggle.

buildLog.title: SEZE rebrand
buildLog.summary: Real brand infinity logo (extracted transparent white/pink PNGs), Poppins Bold wordmark, exact palette (#E04486 / #15B5A6), favicon + app icons + manifest, photo-rich rebuild of every screen using real model photography, unified vector-icon library (emojis removed), Train/Eat/[Home ∞]/Progress/Me nav.
buildLog.verified: Logo, splash, auth, home, programmes verified in-browser; matches brand pack.

verifications.evidence: 560-item runtime DB (526 curated + bundled fallback, deduped). Spot-checks vs known values: tostada 231, banana 107, Big Mac 563, KFC Zinger 450, chicken breast 214, Starbucks Caramel Frappuccino 380. 'big mac and medium fries' now resolves to McDonald's Big Mac 563 + McDonald's Medium Fries 337 (was cheese sauce + BK fries). '2 big apples' still uses size-word path -> apple x2.8. No external API calls.
New auth.users row; trigger handle_new_user() auto-created rosa_users with full_name + consent metadata.
Reads rosa_users.onboarding_complete; new user routed to onboarding, not home.
Upsert wrote life_stage, goals, programme_id, height/weight, targets. New-mum got 2450kcal (lactation +330, NOT a deficit).
3 items written to rosa_nutrition_logs with chosen meal_type=lunch; searched Starbucks Frappuccino logged at 380kcal.
'starbucks caramel' returned curated Frappuccino (SEZE) top, then Open Food Facts results.
'Start this programme' persisted programme_id new_mum→booty; all screens follow it.
New account shows 0 sessions / 0.0 avg / 0% / no badges (was inheriting demo data).
Anon submit HTTP 201; impersonation attempt 401; anon read of others' feedback empty.
Admin sees all; status change to 'reviewed' persisted in Postgres.
admin_overview / admin_users return 'not authorised' for anon; plan toggle persisted (free→trial + start date).
rosa_daily_nutrition view set security_invoker=on; anon can no longer read all users' totals.

risks.risk: No real workout content beyond the built rotation; programmes now have distinct sessions but not full multi-week curricula with form video
risks.mitigation: Build per-programme week-by-week content + demo video before public launch.

risks.risk: No pre-workout medical-clearance gate for pregnancy / early postnatal
risks.mitigation: Add a clearance confirmation + physician-advised flag before heavy loading for perinatal stages.

RESOLVED 2026-07-20: nutrition is now a curated, macro-validated local DB (foods.json, 526 items) served as a static asset. No Open Food Facts / USDA / DEMO_KEY dependency. Trade-off: coverage is bounded to the curated set; unknown foods fall back to a labelled flat estimate. Grow the DB as gaps surface.

risks.risk: No payment provider — 'paying' plan is set manually
risks.mitigation: Wire Stripe; hook its webhook to admin_set_plan; add MRR/churn to the admin panel.
Enable HaveIBeenPwned + min length 8-10 + Turnstile in Supabase Auth (dashboard toggles).

risks.risk: Consent/age captured client-side in editable metadata
risks.mitigation: Persist immutable timestamped consent server-side with policy version; add age assurance.

risks.risk: Branded auth emails / deliverability not configured (e.g. @seze.app mailboxes)
risks.mitigation: Configure custom SMTP + branded SEZE templates in Supabase Auth.

risks.risk: CDN + in-browser Babel, single 3.7k-line file, no offline runtime
risks.mitigation: Precompile to a bundled build + service worker as feature velocity grows.

risks.risk: Vercel build queue can stall (observed 12-23min queued vs usual 2-6s)
risks.mitigation: Use a prebuilt deploy to bypass the queue: vercel build --prod && vercel deploy --prebuilt --prod.

roadmap.item: Perinatal medical-clearance gate before heavy loading
Per-programme multi-week content + exercise demo video
Stripe payments → auto plan updates + revenue metrics
USDA API key + wire barcode scan; log fibre/sugar/sodium
Quick-track Water/Mood/Weight UI → rosa_daily_logs / rosa_body_stats (tables ready)
Account settings: edit profile, change goal, notifications, set-new-password
Brand-value features: progress photos, cycle awareness, pelvic-floor guidance, sleep/recovery, community
Harden for scale: leaked-password protection, CAPTCHA, server-side consent, branded SMTP, precompiled build

decisions.decision: AI/LLM deferred for the testing phase
decisions.rationale: Keep the fast local food parser + real DB lookup now; wire an LLM later. No Railway needed — the analyze-image Edge Function already exists.

decisions.decision: Nutrition data = Open Food Facts + USDA + curated fast-food table
decisions.rationale: Free, no scraping fragility; curated table gives accurate UK/US chain items; OFF/USDA cover the long tail.

decisions.decision: Matcher prioritises life stage over goal, but users can switch
decisions.rationale: A postnatal body shouldn't be dropped into heavy glute loading; but 'Start this programme' now lets the user override and persists it.

decisions.decision: Profile table is rosa_users, not profiles
decisions.rationale: The app wrote to a non-existent 'profiles' table — the real table (with the signup trigger + RLS) is rosa_users. All reads/writes realigned.

decisions.decision: Admin gated by a rosa_admins allowlist + SECURITY DEFINER RPCs
decisions.rationale: Data stays safe even if the UI leaks; add/remove admins with one SQL insert; check is case-insensitive.

decisions.decision: Anon Supabase key in the frontend is safe
decisions.rationale: It is the public anon key; every table's RLS is auth.uid()-scoped and was verified correct.

infra.Tables (public): rosa_users (profile), rosa_sessions, rosa_nutrition_logs, rosa_body_stats, rosa_badges, rosa_feedback, rosa_daily_logs, rosa_food_items, rosa_admins
infra.RLS: Enabled on all; own-row auth.uid() scoping; admins read all via is_seze_admin()
infra.Edge functions: analyze-image (photo/menu; LLM deferred), nutrition-search (curated + Open Food Facts + USDA)
infra.Auth: Email/password + Google active; Apple/Meta 'coming soon'; signup trigger creates the profile row
infra.Admin RPCs: admin_overview, admin_users, admin_requests, admin_set_plan — each re-checks is_seze_admin()
